content_markdown: The solar constant is the intensity of the Sun's radiation at

options.label: B
options.text: the mean distance from the Sun of the Earth's orbit around the Sun.

options.label: D
options.text: $10\text{ km}$ above the surface of the Earth.

vibe_explanation: The solar constant is defined as the power per unit area (intensity) of solar radiation incident on the upper atmosphere of the Earth. This corresponds to the intensity at the average distance of the Earth's orbit from the Sun, before any atmospheric absorption or scattering occurs.
